regulation_clause.text: Failure on the part of the card-issuers to complete the process of closure within seven working days shall result in a penalty of ₹500 per day of delay payable to the customer, till the closure of the account provided there is no outstanding in the account.

gold_rationale: Penalty accrues on working days; defensible under the 2022 unqualified 'Rs 500 per day of delay' with the SLA itself in working days.

provenance.annotator_notes: P1 old side; defensible-ambiguous stance per registry.

regulation_clause.text: Closure requests must be honoured within 7 working days (subject to dues); failure incurs a penalty of Rs 500 per calendar day of delay until closure, provided no outstanding.

gold_rationale: 2025 para 19 fixes accrual to calendar days; this code subtracts weekends/holidays from accrual days, undercharging the mandated penalty.

provenance.annotator_notes: P1 new side; identical code_locus to previous instance.

regulation_clause.text: Failure on the part of the card-issuers to complete the process of closure within seven working days shall result in a penalty of ₹500 per day of delay payable to the customer, till the closure of the account provided there is no outstanding in the account.

gold_rationale: Day basis site-configurable via copybook WSDAYBAS (working days); defensible under 2022 wording.

provenance.annotator_notes: P1x old side; interprocedural: basis constant defined in copybook.

regulation_clause.text: Closure requests must be honoured within 7 working days (subject to dues); failure incurs a penalty of Rs 500 per calendar day of delay until closure, provided no outstanding.

gold_rationale: 2025 para 19 mandates calendar-day accrual; copybook ships WS-DAY-BASIS='W' and 2000-CALC branches on it to subtract weekends - stale constant far from use site.

provenance.annotator_notes: P1x new side; MO-1x shape occurring naturally.

regulation_clause.text: If a complainant does not get satisfactory response from the card-issuer within a maximum period of one month from the date of lodging the complaint, he/she will have the option to approach the Office of the concerned RBI Ombudsman.

gold_rationale: Due date = complaint month + 1: faithful to the 2022 'maximum period of one month'.

regulation_clause.text: If a complainant does not get a satisfactory response within a maximum period of 30 days from lodging the complaint, they may approach the RBI Ombudsman.

gold_rationale: 2025 para 85 fixes 30 days; month+1 yields 28-31 days - complaints in 31-day months escalate a day late.

provenance.annotator_notes: P3 new side; differential narrow by design (month boundaries).

regulation_clause.text: Where the customer is a partnership firm, the beneficial owner is the natural person(s), who, whether acting alone or together, or through one or more juridical person, has/have ownership of/entitlement to more than 15 per cent of capital or profits of the partnership.

gold_rationale: BO at >15 per cent: exactly the 2016 MD 3(a)(iv)(b) rule in force until 2023-10-16.

regulation_clause.text: For partnership firms, the beneficial owner is the natural person with ownership of/entitlement to more than 10 percent of capital or profits (lowered from 15 percent by the 2023-10-17 amendment to the 2016 MD).

gold_rationale: Current rule: >10 per cent AND the control-through-other-means limb; code uses 15.00 with no control branch - compound drift.

provenance.annotator_notes: P4 new side; compound delta - pairing logic must not assume single-field diffs.

regulation_clause.text: ABSENCE (old side is the absence of any update-deadline): clause (h) required uploading/updating KYC data at periodic updation or when updated information was received, with NO time-bound deadline. The seven-day sentence carries footnote 148: 'Inserted vide circular DOR.AML.REC.49/14.01.001/2024-25 dated November 6, 2024' - 'Inserted', not 'Amended': the sentence had no predecessor in any form.

gold_rationale: Queues CKYCR upload with no deadline: conformant with para 56(h) before 2024-11-06 (footnote 148: sentence 'Inserted' then).

provenance.annotator_notes: P5 old side; absence-form old side.

regulation_clause.text: Regulated entities must update KYC records in the Central KYC Records Registry within 7 days of receiving updated information from a customer.

gold_rationale: 65(8) requires CKYCR update within seven days of receiving updated information; no receipt date tracked, no SLA enforced - check absent (insertion at queue action).

provenance.annotator_notes: P5 new side; D2 insertion-point label.

regulation_clause.text: A card account may be reported past-due to CICs or levied penal charges only when past due for more than three days; days-past-due and late charges are computed from the payment due date; late charges apply only to the outstanding amount after the due date, not the Total Amount Due.

gold_rationale: Late charge computed on statement total (WS-TOTAL-AMT-DUE) instead of outstanding-after-due-date; 23(5)'s base restriction has held since 2022 - drifted under BOTH versions.

provenance.annotator_notes: Conformance-invariant by design (P2 retired). Comments cite repealed MD 2022 - stale-citation realism (P6-adjacent).

regulation_clause.text: Closure requests must be honoured within 7 working days (subject to dues); failure incurs a penalty of Rs 500 per calendar day of delay until closure, provided no outstanding.

gold_rationale: Closure processor never computes the Rs 500 per-calendar-day penalty required by para 19 - rule entirely absent (insertion at the TBD marker).

provenance.annotator_notes: Shipped 'TBD' comment marks the gap - mirrors CardDemo's ADD MORE VALIDATIONS HERE.

regulation_clause.text: Closure requests must be honoured within 7 working days (subject to dues); failure incurs a penalty of Rs 500 per calendar day of delay until closure, provided no outstanding.

gold_rationale: 2000-PENALTY is correct but unreachable: WS-PEN-ENABLED initializes 'N' and nothing sets it - compliance code dead behind a pilot flag.

provenance.annotator_notes: Cross-paragraph single-program case: is_interprocedural covers it per schema comment. T2.7 moved this real dead-code example to the pinned disabled-state host while restoring CLOSPEN5 as the synthetic MO-6 pre-mutation base.

regulation_clause.text: Closure requests must be honoured within 7 working days (subject to dues); failure incurs a penalty of Rs 500 per calendar day of delay until closure, provided no outstanding.

gold_rationale: SLA in working days, accrual per calendar day at Rs 500 - matches para 19 on both dimensions.

regulation_clause.text: Regulated entities must update KYC records in the Central KYC Records Registry within 7 days of receiving updated information from a customer.

gold_rationale: Flags upload overdue past 7 days since updated information - implements 65(8).

regulation_clause.text: Periodic updation of KYC shall be carried out at least once every two years for high-risk, once every eight years for medium-risk, and once every ten years for low-risk customers, from the date of account opening or last KYC updation.

gold_rationale: Risk-branched 2/8/10-year updation matches 42(1); values stable across the lineage (future MO-1 host).

regulation_clause.text: For partnership firms, the beneficial owner is the natural person with ownership of/entitlement to more than 10 percent of capital or profits (lowered from 15 percent by the 2023-10-17 amendment to the 2016 MD).

gold_rationale: Threshold 10.00 plus the control limb - implements current 5(iv)(b) including the compound 2023 change.

regulation_clause.text: If a card is unused for more than one year, initiate closure after intimating the cardholder; if no reply within 30 days, close the account (subject to dues) and update the CIC within 30 days.

gold_rationale: Posting validator never reads ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS: transactions post to closed/blocked accounts - required status rule absent (insertion at 'ADD MORE VALIDATIONS HERE').

regulation_clause.text: The sanctioned credit limit shall not be breached at any point in time without explicit consent from the cardholder.

gold_rationale: 12(5) permits limit breach only with explicit consent; no consent dimension exists anywhere in the validation chain (insertion at the limit IF).

provenance.annotator_notes: Code is STRICTER than the clause but the required consent mechanism is absent - annotation-guideline stance (T5.1). Cross-paragraph locus.

regulation_clause.text: The sanctioned credit limit shall not be breached at any point in time without explicit consent from the cardholder.

gold_rationale: Projected-balance-vs-limit gate correctly enforces the no-breach core of 12(5).

provenance.annotator_notes: Conformant on the limit portion; consent gap is the separate instance. Different locus shape - not a pair.

regulation_clause.text: Interest shall be levied only on the outstanding amount, adjusted for payments, refunds and reversed transactions.

gold_rationale: Interest per transaction-category posted balance - consistent with 23(6) interest-only-on-outstanding.

provenance.annotator_notes: Caveat: 23(2) fee-category capitalization NOT verified; conformance asserted for 23(6) base selection only.

regulation_clause.text: Failure on the part of the card-issuers to complete the process of closure within seven working days shall result in a penalty of ₹500 per day of delay payable to the customer, till the closure of the account provided there is no outstanding in the account.

gold_rationale: The same code subtracts recorded weekends and holidays before accrual. That working-day accrual is defensible under 2022 clause 8(a), whose seven-day service window and unqualified per-day wording did not yet prescribe calendar-day penalty accrual.

regulation_clause.text: Closure requests must be honoured within 7 working days (subject to dues); failure incurs a penalty of Rs 500 per calendar day of delay until closure, provided no outstanding.

gold_rationale: The same code subtracts recorded weekends and holidays before accrual, excluding non-working days from penalty accrual. Current paragraph 19 expressly requires Rs 500 per calendar day, so the unchanged implementation undercharges.

regulation_clause.text: Failure on the part of the card-issuers to complete the process of closure within seven working days shall result in a penalty of ₹500 per day of delay payable to the customer, till the closure of the account provided there is no outstanding in the account.

gold_rationale: The same code counts only day-table entries marked as working days. That working-day accrual is defensible under 2022 clause 8(a), whose seven-day service window and unqualified per-day wording did not yet prescribe calendar-day penalty accrual.

regulation_clause.text: Where the customer is a partnership firm, the beneficial owner is the natural person(s), who, whether acting alone or together, or through one or more juridical person, has/have ownership of/entitlement to more than 15 per cent of capital or profits of the partnership.

gold_rationale: The same code compares capital ownership directly with 15 percent. That implements the 2016 partnership beneficial-owner threshold of more than 15 percent.

regulation_clause.text: For partnership firms, the beneficial owner is the natural person with ownership of/entitlement to more than 10 percent of capital or profits (lowered from 15 percent by the 2023-10-17 amendment to the 2016 MD).

gold_rationale: The same code compares capital ownership directly with 15 percent. Current clause 5(iv)(b) lowered the threshold to more than 10 percent and includes control through other means, so the unchanged legacy rule misses required owners.

regulation_clause.text: Where the customer is a partnership firm, the beneficial owner is the natural person(s), who, whether acting alone or together, or through one or more juridical person, has/have ownership of/entitlement to more than 15 per cent of capital or profits of the partnership.

gold_rationale: The same code compares profit share with a stored 15-percent legacy limit. That implements the 2016 partnership beneficial-owner threshold of more than 15 percent.

regulation_clause.text: For partnership firms, the beneficial owner is the natural person with ownership of/entitlement to more than 10 percent of capital or profits (lowered from 15 percent by the 2023-10-17 amendment to the 2016 MD).

gold_rationale: The same code compares profit share with a stored 15-percent legacy limit. Current clause 5(iv)(b) lowered the threshold to more than 10 percent and includes control through other means, so the unchanged legacy rule misses required owners.

regulation_clause.text: ABSENCE (old side is the absence of any update-deadline): clause (h) required uploading/updating KYC data at periodic updation or when updated information was received, with NO time-bound deadline. The seven-day sentence carries footnote 148: 'Inserted vide circular DOR.AML.REC.49/14.01.001/2024-25 dated November 6, 2024' - 'Inserted', not 'Amended': the sentence had no predecessor in any form.

gold_rationale: The same code queues a received update without retaining elapsed days. Before 2024-11-06, para 56(h) required CKYCR updating but imposed no time-bound deadline, so this deadline-free workflow is conformant to that temporal side.

regulation_clause.text: Regulated entities must update KYC records in the Central KYC Records Registry within 7 days of receiving updated information from a customer.

gold_rationale: The same code queues a received update without retaining elapsed days. Current clause 65(8) requires update within seven days of receipt; this unchanged workflow has no receipt date or deadline enforcement, so the missing rule is drift.
